channel.title: Yahoo News – World
channel.description: Latest world news from Yahoo News

item.title: Colombia's presidential election, in photos
item.description: Source: AP
item.dc:creator: AP

item.title: Israel seizes Crusader castle in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli forces seized a 12th-century Crusader castle in southern Lebanon, as Israel intensifies its farthest incursion into the country in 26 years, despite a nominal ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Semafor

item.title: Israeli forces push further into south Lebanon, seize Beaufort castle
item.description: Israeli troops advanced deeper into Lebanon on Sunday, seizing the medieval Beaufort castle for the first time in 26 years amid Hezbollah attacks.
item.dc:creator: UPI

item.title: Analysis: Lebanon's Shiites face a 'semi-Nakba,' bearing brunt of war
item.description: Lebanon's Muslim Shiite community, whose fortunes rose with the once-powerful Hezbollah, is facing what many are likening it to a "semi-Nakba."
item.dc:creator: UPI

item.title: Israel expands Lebanon offensive after capturing historic castle
item.description: Israeli troops are moving further into Lebanon after capturing a historic fortress, despite an ongoing ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: USA TODAY

item.title: Congo says number of confirmed Ebola cases rises to 282
item.description: KINSHASA, May 31 (Reuters) - Democratic Republic of Congo said on Sunday that the number of confirmed Ebola cases had increased to 282 after 19 new positive test results were recorded.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Zelenskyy makes a pitch to Silicon Valley's defense startups: Bring your AI, we'll bring the battle experience
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy wants to deepen ties between Ukraine and Silicon Valley's defense tech startups.
item.dc:creator: Business Insider

item.title: Nicaragua confirms death in custody of Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera
item.description: Rivera spent nearly three years in detention, cut off from the outside world, leading advocates to fear for his welfare.

item.title: Man Trampled to Death by Rampaging Wild Elephant Outside His Home
item.description: The fatal incident was the most recent in a string of elephant attacks in southern India

item.title: At Least 5 People Dead, 3 Missing After WWII-Era Bomb Explodes, Destroying Several Homes
item.description: The search for the individuals reported missing will resume on June 1, as authorities continue to investigate the destructive May 31 blast

item.title: Nicaraguan indigenous leader dies after three years in prison
item.description: Rights groups condemn the death of Brooklyn Rivera, 73, following his long arbitrary detention by the governing regime.

item.title: African Countries With The Highest Fertility Rates Ranked
item.description: World Bank data reveals the African nations topping global birth charts-and the surprising outlier where having kids is becoming rare.

item.title: Fear grips residents of Ugandan town over Ebola outbreak
item.description: On a hot afternoon in a hospital in the town of Bundibugyo, health workers were being trained to fight Ebola. This particular day, they were astounded when dozens of patients suddenly scattered, some fearfully leaping over the benches and hedges to safety.

item.title: Macron vows response after clashes mar PSG's Champions League win
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has condemned the riots that broke out nationwide following Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League victory over Arsenal, vowing a tough response. "That's not soccer, that's not sports, that's not what we love," Macron said while receiving the French side at the Élysée Palace following their return from Budapest, according to French broadcaster BFMTV.

item.title: Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera dies in Nicaragua after nearly 3 years of detention
item.description: Brooklyn Rivera, a renowned Indigenous leader from Nicaragua who spent years fighting for the rights of his community and was imprisoned by the government in September 2023, has died. The Nicaraguan government issued a statement Sunday saying that Rivera died from a bacterial infection after his health had declined following a case of COVID-19, which led to his physical and neurological deterioration. Human rights activists and groups worldwide denounced his death and a previous statement by the government in which they referred to Rivera as “Brother” and said they were praying for him.

item.title: Car ramming attack wounds several at Gush Etzion Junction in West Bank, south of Jerusalem
item.description: The soldier who killed the terrorist also provided emergency medical treatment to one of the wounded, saving their life, a spokesperson from the Gush Etzion Regional Council said. A terrorist was killed on Sunday evening after a car ramming attack at the Gush Etzion Junction, West Bank.

item.title: Malta PM Abela secures new term as Labour Party wins elections
item.description: Malta's governing Labour Party (MLP) has won the EU country's general election, paving the way for a second term for Prime Minister Robert Abela, official results showed on Sunday evening. The MLP garnered 51.

item.title: Meet the Israeli-Bedouin women’s rights activist bringing women to the negotiation table
item.description: “Stubborn” was how the Israeli Bedouin women’s rights activist and lawyer described herself in an interview with The Jerusalem Post on Sunday. Whenever Hanan Alsanah was told she could not do something, she did it anyway.

item.title: German top diplomat concerned over Israeli advance in Lebanon
item.description: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul on Sunday called on Israel and Hezbollah to return to the ceasefire, as hostilities escalated again in Lebanon. Wadephul said the Israeli military's "continued advance" into southern Lebanon was "a cause for great concern.

item.title: Dead whale towed ashore in Denmark ahead of autopsy
item.description: "Timmy" was found dead off the coast of Anholt despite multiple attempts to rescue it.

item.title: Diver details "heartwarming" moments after miners emerged from Laos cave
item.description: Lead rescue diver Mikko Paasi said it took him a moment to realize the four trapped miners had "self-rescued."

item.title: Nicaraguan Indigenous leader imprisoned under Ortega dies in detention
item.description: Nicaraguan Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera died from health complications after nearly three years in detention and despite US appeals for his release, the health ministry said Sunday.Rivera was a leader of the Indigenous party Yatama (Children of Mother Earth United), which defends the rights of Nicaragua's native communities.

item.title: Ukraine strikes Russian oil pumping station 800 miles behind front lines
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck a Russian fuel depot, oil pumping station and a refinery in an escalating campaign of strikes against energy infrastructure often hundreds of miles inside Russia.

item.title: Zelensky says he hopes Witkoff, Kushner visit Kyiv for talks
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Sunday said he hopes White House special envoy Steve Witkoff and President Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner, will come to Kyiv, Ukraine, for talks in the coming weeks. CBS News’s “Face the Nation” host Margaret Brennan asked the president whether he had heard from the envoys and Secretary of State Marco…

item.title: Man Gored to Death and Another Injured in 2 Separate Buffalo Attacks During Religious Sacrifice Rituals
item.description: Both incidents occurred during celebrations for Eid al-Adha, one of Islam's holiest days

item.title: At least four people reportedly wounded after Hezbollah drone hits northern Israel
item.description: Fire crews were working at the crash site, where Israeli media is reporting that at least four people were injured. No details on their conditions have been reported.

item.title: Brazil monitors two patients for possible Ebola infection
item.description: If confirmed, they would be the first infection cases outside Africa, since the outbreak began in DR Congo.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Apologizes For Catholic Church Slavery Role
item.description: In a sweeping encyclical on AI and human dignity, the first U.S.-born pope confronts centuries of Catholic complicity in the slave trade-and asks for forgiveness.

item.title: Russia losing initiative on battlefield in Ukraine, says Zelensky
item.description: Russia is losing the initiative on the battlefield in its war against Ukraine, paving the way for a diplomatic solution to the conflict, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Sunday. In an interview with US broadcaster CBS, Zelensky said the Russian army has been increasingly on the back foot since the end of December, suffering heavy losses.

item.title: 3 Climbers Killed In Tragedy at Mount McKinley
item.description: Tragedy has struck a climbing expedition at a U.S. national park in Alaska this week. Three climbers have been found dead at Mount McKinley in Alaska. The three climbers were members of a Latvian climbing expedition, according to the National Park Service and the Latvian Mountaineering Association. ...

item.title: Over 40 dead in Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda
item.description: Over 40 people have died in a growing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda, where government and international aid organizations are struggling to cope with the spread of the disease

item.title: Rescuers say a blast at a building storing explosives in Myanmar has killed more than 45 people
item.description: A blast on Sunday at a building in northeastern Myanmar said to have been storing explosives for mining has killed more than 45 people, according to rescue workers and independent media reports. The area, located about 3 kilometers (2 miles) south of the Chinese border, is under the control of the Ta’ang National Liberation Army, an ethnic armed group which has engaged in sporadic fighting against Myanmar’s central government. Another rescuer in Namhkam, who also spoke on condition of anonymity, said about 40 people were killed and more than 100 houses near the blast site were damaged.

item.title: Nicaraguan Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera dies in state custody, media reports say
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: 8 Dead, Including 9-Month-Old Boy and His Dad, After Tourist Bus Crashes into Highway Barriers and Bursts into Flames
item.description: More than 30 people were also injured in the accident

item.title: French museum reports theft of arty banana
item.description: A museum in eastern France on Sunday reported to police the theft of a banana that forms a core part of a multimillion-dollar artwork by Italian visual artist Maurizio Cattelan.The missing fruit -- which was taped to a wall to form the provocative work by Cattelan called "Comedian" -- was noticed by a guard on Saturday to have gone missing.

item.title: Zelenskyy alleges Russia is abducting Ukrainian kids, training them to fight
item.description: This is the first time Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has made this accusation, which may constitute a war crime.

item.title: Italy bans Kanye West concert over security concerns
item.description: A concert by the US rapper Kanye West, who was supposed to perform on July 18 in Reggio Emilia in northern Italy, has been banned on public safety grounds, the authorities said.West performed in Istanbul on Saturday and is still due to appear at concerts in the Netherlands on June 6 and 8, in Tirana on July 11, and Prague on July 25.

item.title: Huge blast kills dozens in rebel-held village in Myanmar
item.description: Insurgents say it was caused by explosives being used for mining close to the Chinese border.

item.title: Iran ⁠restores some gas production at South Pars facility
item.description: Production restored at three offshore platforms after Israeli attacks, Pars Oil and Gas Company CEO tells Iranian media.

item.title: Police arrest 780 in Paris soccer team victory riots
item.description: Police in France arrested hundreds of people late Saturday after celebrations for the Paris Saint-Germain soccer team turned violent.

item.title: Africa CDC says over 1,100 suspected Ebola cases in DR Congo and Uganda
item.description: More than 1,100 people were suspected of having contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo and neighbouring Uganda, the head of the African Union's health agency said on Sunday in a commentary for the Financial Times.More than 1,100 suspected cases were still being investigated, he wrote in the editorial.

item.title: IAEA: Radiation levels normal at Zaporizhzhya plant after drone strike
item.description: Radiation levels at Ukraine's Russian-occupied Zaporizhzhya nuclear power plant "remain normal" following a suspected drone strike, the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) said on Sunday. IAEA experts stationed at the plant were able to inspect the exterior of the affected turbine hall on Sunday, according to the UN's nuclear watchdog.

item.title: At least 50 reportedly killed in blast at Myanmar explosives depot
item.description: At least 50 people have been killed in a blast at an explosives depot in north-eastern Myanmar, local media reported on Sunday. A further 70 people were injured in the incident in the town of Namhkam in Shan State, near the Chinese border, according to initial numbers provided by emergency services.

item.title: Islamic regime shuts down cafe in Tehran following accusations of 'promoting satanic activities'
item.description: The cafe was said to have allowed events featuring Western-style music that “provided a setting for abnormal behaviors,” the semi-state official Fars news agency reported. The Islamic regime forced a cafe in central Tehran to close over allegations the business promoted “satanic” activities, according to state-affiliated media reports on Sunday.

item.title: Major Pat Gardner, Marine awarded an MC in Malaya who was later a vigorously active philanthropist
item.description: Major Pat Gardner, RM, who has died aged 96, was awarded the MC in the Malaya Emergency, and 11 years later was Mentioned in Despatches during Konfrontasi, the war in Borneo; his Christian faith later brought him to philanthropy in England and South Africa.

item.title: IAEA: Radiation levels normal at Zaporizhzhya plant after drone strike
item.description: Radiation levels at Ukraine's Russian-occupied Zaporizhzhya nuclear power plant "remain normal" following a suspected drone strike, the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) said on Sunday. IAEA experts stationed at the plant were able to inspect the exterior of the affected turbine hall on Sunday, according to the UN's nuclear watchdog.

item.title: Brazil monitors two patients for Ebola amid fears of spread from Africa
item.description: Brazil is monitoring two patients for possible Ebola infection, officials said, prompting further concern of the deadly virus outbreak in central Africa spreading abroad.

item.title: Palestinian man is shot and killed at a West Bank barrier near Jerusalem
item.description: A Palestinian man was shot and killed by Israeli forces on Sunday at a concrete barrier separating the occupied West Bank from Jerusalem, the Palestinian Health Ministry said. The ministry identified him as 26-year-old Imad Haroun Ishtayeh from Salem town, east of Nablus. It said Israeli forces shot him in a thigh in al-Ram town, and he was pronounced dead at the Palestinian Medical Complex in Ramallah.

item.title: Fireworks send dozens of horses bolting through central Rome
item.description: Dozens of horses bolted through the streets of Rome, leaving several riders injured, after a traffic officer set off fireworks.

item.title: Israel raises flag over historic Lebanese castle
item.description: Israel has captured a Crusader castle in southern Lebanon, as it warned more civilians in the south of the country to evacuate their homes.

item.title: Zelensky angers Warsaw by naming unit after group that killed Poles
item.description: Volodymyr Zelensky’s decree renaming a Ukrainian unit after a group from the Second World War has sparked a bitter row between Kyiv and Warsaw.

item.title: Attack on Europe’s Largest Nuclear Power Plant ‘Playing With Fire,’ UN Says
item.description: The Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine has been controlled by Russia for more than four years.

item.title: Pastor convicted for preaching John 3:16 near hospital files appeal, warns of free speech precedent
item.description: A 78-year-old retired pastor appealed his conviction for preaching a Bible sermon near a Northern Ireland hospital abortion services buffer zone.

item.title: Colombians weigh leftist reforms against right-wing crackdowns in presidential vote
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: At Least 5 Theme Park Goers Injured After Swing Ride Topples Over, Leaving Riders Crashing to the Ground
item.description: “The wear and tear of the machine parts seems to have played a role in the accident,” a police official said, per a local report

item.title: Iran reinstates some internet access but restrictions remain for most
item.description: Not all data centres are back online while internet protocols remain blocked, restricted or ‘whitelisted’.

item.title: Grandson Was Helping Grandma Clean Out Her Garage. Then He Found WWI Explosive Shells, and the Bomb Squad Was Called
item.description: "It was quite funny to be honest — me and my nan were just holding them in the garden," George Cheetham said of the devices

item.title: Israeli forces make historic push inside Lebanon and complicate an Iran deal
item.description: Israeli forces are making their deepest incursion inside Lebanon since they withdrew from the country over a quarter-century ago, despite a nominal U.S.-brokered ceasefire and the first direct Israel-Lebanon talks in decades. It's a challenge in the emerging deal to extend the Iran war ceasefire as Tehran wants it to end fighting in Lebanon, too. On Sunday, Israeli forces seized a symbolic fort in southern Lebanon, Beaufort castle, perched at over 700 meters (3,000 feet) above sea level.

item.title: How Britain’s disability diagnosis epidemic reached Disneyland
item.description: It’s a sweltering morning at Disneyland Paris and the queue to collect a disabled pass is barely budging. Most of the people in the queue look and sound British: one is holding a letter from the Department of Work and Pensions (DWP); many are wearing sunflower lanyards to indicate that their disability is invisible. Behind me, I hear an English woman complaining that the whole point of getting a disability pass is not to have to queue. And yet we inch along.

item.title: English cops cuffed teen stabbing victim after attacker claimed racial assault
item.description: Police apologize after handcuffing stabbing victim Henry Nowak based on attacker's alleged racism claim, sparking outrage over British policing culture.

item.title: Veteran, 101, helps mark Polish allies anniversary
item.description: Guests at Checkendon camp celebrated the 80th anniversary of allied Polish Troops arriving.

item.title: Suspicious letter sent to Norwegian princess at Sydney university
item.description: An Australian man has been banned from contacting Norway's Princess Ingrid Alexandra, who is currently studying in Sydney, after police intercepted a suspicious letter sent to campus, Norwegian media reported on Sunday. Security personnel working for the princess intercepted the letter addressed to her, Norwegian news agency NTB reported.

item.title: World Health Organization hails recovery of five Ebola patients
item.description: More than 220 people are suspected to have died from the disease’s latest outbreak, which was declared two weeks ago.

item.title: Israeli forces push past Lebanon’s Litani River: How significant is it?
item.description: Analysts say Israel is effectively expanding its area of military control northward towards the Zahrani River.

item.title: Satellite imagery shows erasure of southern Gaza as Israel expands control
item.description: Satellite images show transformation as Netanyahu orders military to occupy 70 percent of Gaza.

item.title: Iran War Again Underlined Iraqi Kurdistan’s Critical Air Defense Need
item.description: While it’s arguably clearer than ever that Iraqi Kurdistan urgently needs air defenses, it remains equally unclear where or how it can get them.

item.title: France calls for emergency UN meeting on Israel's advance in Lebanon
item.description: France on Sunday called for an emergency meeting of the UN Security Council as Israeli forces advanced further into southern Lebanon, seizing a historic landmark as a base for expanded attacks on Hezbollah. “Nothing can justify the continuation of Israeli military operations in Lebanon and an ever-deeper occupation of Lebanese territory,” Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot told the French broadcaster BFMTV.

item.title: Leo Shows He’s the Planet’s Pope by Taking on AI
item.description: The leader of the Catholic Church called artificial intelligence a threat to humanity in his first encyclical

item.title: Hot-air balloons take to the skies at festival
item.description: The three-day event at Alcester also includes aerobatic displays from the world-famous Red Arrows.

item.title: Mouse plague terrorises Australian farms
item.description: A plague of mice is devastating fields and invading homes in parts of rural Australia.

item.title: Japan Denies ‘New Militarism’ After China Claims
item.description: Japanese Defense Minister Shinjirō Koizumi said on Sunday that the "door is always open" to China.

item.title: ‘The arteries of modern civilization’: The US and allies take action to protect seabed cables
item.description: The United States, Australia and the United Kingdom have taken a big step towards tackling growing threats to undersea pipelines and cables, which carry huge amounts of energy and data around the world.

item.title: D-Day memorial to British soldiers stolen
item.description: A bronze statue commemorating British soldiers who fought on D-Day has been stolen from a French town in Normandy.

item.title: Carlo Petrini, founder of the Slow Food movement who championed sustainability and local cultures
item.description: Carlo Petrini, who has died aged 76 in his home town of Bra in Piedmont, was the founder of the Slow Food movement, whose mission is to create localised tradition-based alternatives to globalised fast food and the tyranny of supermarkets.

item.title: France detains close to 800 in unrest after PSG Champions League win
item.description: Hundreds of people were detained and dozens of police officers injured across France following Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League victory over Arsenal, as celebrations in several cities turned into unrest. Releasing updated figures, French Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez said on Sunday that 780 people had been detained nationwide, including some 480 in the Paris metropolitan area.

item.title: Pupils identified as arsonists in deadly school fire in Kenya
item.description: Following the deadly fire at a girls’ boarding school in Kenya, police on Sunday identified seven pupils as the arsonists using the school’s CCTV footage. No information has yet emerged regarding a possible motive.

item.title: Israel captures strategic fortress during advance in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli troops on Sunday claimed to have taken control of the strategically located Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon for the first time in 26 years during ground operations against the Hezbollah militia. Defence Minister Israel Katz said troops had raised the Israeli colours on the highland stronghold, which an army spokesman described as a "symbol of Hezbollah’s arrogance.

item.title: Volunteers rehearse life-saving disaster response
item.description: A simulated building collapse helps volunteers to prepare for global search and rescue missions.

item.title: Full transcript: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 31, 2026
item.description: The following is the full transcript of the interview with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, a portion of which aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 31, 2026. The interview was taped on May 29, 2026.

item.title: Return to Beaufort: Israel’s symbolic conquest of a Lebanese Crusader fortress
item.description: Now the IDF is back at the Beaufort, but this time there is a sense of conquest. Israel has changed a lot in the last two decades.

item.title: Nigerian retired general abducted with his wife in the north-west
item.description: Maj Gen Rabe Abubakar's kidnapping highlights continuing security challenges facing parts of the country.

item.title: Psychic Soldiers, Mind Readers, and Dolphin Drones: The Cold War's Weird Paranormal History
item.description: How Soviet séances and CIA remote viewers sparked a decades-long arms race no one was supposed to know about

item.title: Taiwan condemns China after New York Times reporter expelled after presidential interview
item.description: TAIPEI, May 31 (Reuters) - Taiwan's presidential office condemned China on Sunday after the New York Times said one of its reporters was expelled from the country following an interview the newspaper

item.title: Laos cave survivors help with plan to find last two missing men
item.description: So far, five men have been freed from the narrow, flooded cave but efforts are ongoing to locate two more.

item.title: Former commander of Beaufort Ridge warns against romanticizing operation, holding outpost
item.description: He warned that those who will pay the price of holding the ridge will be the soldiers and Israeli citizens living in the north. Brig.

item.title: Israel Tax Authority official questioned by Lahav 433 over alleged privacy, misconduct offenses
item.description: A few months ago, the senior official allegedly locked the door of his office while an employee was inside, confiscated the employee's mobile phone, and attempted to review recordings. A deputy director-general at the Israel Tax Authority was questioned byLahav 433 investigators on Sunday over suspicions of multiple offenses, including invasion of privacy, unlawful detention, obstruction of justice, fraud, and breach of trust.

item.title: In pictures: IDF retakes southern Lebanon's Beaufort Ridge outpost nearly three decades later
item.description: The IDF held the outpost until May 2000, when Israel withdrew its forces from southern Lebanon on the order of former prime minister Ehud Barak. IDF shares footage of operation in Beaufort Ridge, May 31, 2026.

item.title: Malta PM Abela set for new term as opposition concedes election
item.description: Malta's governing Labour Party (MLP) has declared victory in the EU country's general election. "We have made history by winning for the fourth time in a row," said Prime Minister Robert Abela in a television interview on Sunday after initial results showed his party leading the conservative Nationalist Party (PN).

item.title: Zelensky thanks Germany for Iris-T air defence system launch pad
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Sunday that Ukraine has received a new launch pad for the Iris-T air defence system from Germany. "We thank Germany for its contribution to the protection of our people," he wrote on Telegram, saying thousands of lives had been saved thanks to Berlin's "significant assistance.

item.title: Ukraine has a war lesson for NATO forces: Drone units need to be constantly on the move with command centers buried deep
item.description: Drone command centers, units, and operators are highly valuable targets, so Ukraine keeps them mobile and underground.

item.title: How the Laos cave survivors found the courage to rescue themselves
item.description: Hungry and weak, the Laos cave survivors huddled together in damp darkness for 11 days, clinging to hope as a wall of water blocked their way out.

item.title: Ukraine hits Russian energy targets and denies striking Kremlin-occupied nuclear plant
item.description: Ukraine denied Moscow's claims on Sunday that a Ukrainian drone struck the Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant, as Kyiv launched fresh strikes overnight on Russian energy sites. Ukraine’s General Staff on Sunday said Ukrainian drones had struck the Saratov oil refinery in southwestern Russia, causing a “large-scale fire.” It said the extent of the damage was being clarified, and claimed the refinery has been supplying Moscow’s war effort.

item.title: Don’t let the Paras’ heroism in Northern Ireland be written out of history
item.description: On May 25, 1971, Sergeant Michael Willetts of the Parachute Regiment was killed in Springfield Road Police Station, Belfast, when a Provisional IRA suitcase bomb exploded.

item.title: Israel seizes castle in Lebanon as it expands ground offensive
item.description: The Israeli military says everyone south of the Zahrani river should leave, after it captured the strategic Beaufort Castle.

item.title: WHO chief hails 5 Ebola recoveries as a new treatment center opens in eastern Congo
item.description: Five patients have recovered from a rare type of Ebola, the head of the World Health Organization said Sunday during a visit to eastern Congo’s Bunia, a city at the heart of an outbreak. “Four people will be discharged today and there was one that was discharged the day before yesterday,” WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said during the opening of a new Ebola treatment center in Bunia, the provinical capital of Ituri. “Of course, we’re still working on vaccines and treatments but that doesn’t mean that people cannot recover from Ebola,” he added.

item.title: Petrol shortages in Crimea after Ukrainian drone attacks
item.description: Moscow - Authorities on the Crimean Peninsula - illegally annexed by Russia in 2014 - have introduced restrictions on petrol sales following Ukrainian drone attacks on oil facilities. AI-95 petrol, a premium unleaded type equivalent to 95-octane fuel sold in the European Union, will be prioritized for public and social transport, Crimea's Moscow-appointed governor Sergei Aksyonov posted on Telegram on Saturday.

item.title: IAEA issues Ukraine nuclear plant warning after Russia alleges Zaporizhzhia attack
item.description: The ZNPP sits around 30 miles from the closest portion of the front line.

item.title: O'Sullivan clan gathering in Ireland breaks world record
item.description: Almost 2,000 people with the last names O'Sullivan and Sullivan travelled from around Ireland and the world to meet in County Cork on Saturday, in the largest ever recorded gathering of people with the same surname.  The surnames O'Sullivan and Sullivan are commonplace in County Cork and southwestern Ireland.

item.title: Lebanese PM accuses Israel of 'scorched-earth policy' while defending ceasefire talks
item.description: Salam’s comments came amid the now-revealed IDF operation of crossing the Litani River, where it successfully secured the Beaufort Ridge and Wadi al-Saluki areas of southern Lebanon. Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam accused Israel of pursuing a “scorched-earth policy” as part of its operations against Hezbollah, The Guardian reported on Sunday.

item.title: Two Jerusalem residents indicted for allegedly planning terror attack against IDF soldiers
item.description: In a separate incident, police found the knife used to kill Destao Tsakol, 19, in Beersheba. A prosecutor's statement was filed against the four minors who are suspects in the case.

item.title: Michelin Used To Award Eco-Friendly Green Stars, Now, Some Restaurants Want Them Back
item.description: You may know Michelin as a tire company, or associate it with the prestigious restaurant guide and its culinary stars. But have you heard of the Green Star?

item.title: Israel captures strategic Crusader-built castle as incursion into southern Lebanon deepens
item.description: The Israeli military has captured the Crasader-era Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon, a site it hasn’t held for 26 years, as it expands its incursion deeper into its neighbor’s territory.

item.title: Heavy rains threaten to delay search for 2 people missing in Laos cave
item.description: Heavy rains threatened to delay the search for two people missing in a flooded cave in Laos on Sunday, after the rescue of five other people who were trapped underground for over a week. Finnish diver Mikko Paasi, one of the first international rescuers to arrive at the site, told The Associated Press that rains had filled the cave up to the second chamber, preventing divers from entering the cave until pumps can lower the water level. Rescue teams from Laos and neighboring Thailand have been working together in the past week at the site in a rugged area in the central province of Xaisomboun, about 120 kilometers (75 miles) north of the capital, Vientiane.

item.title: Ballot counting starts in Malta general election
item.description: Malta was to begin counting votes Sunday in an election expected to deliver a historic fourth term for the Labour government, despite concerns over rampant overdevelopment and systemic corruption.Voting took place across the tiny Mediterranean island nation on Saturday, and ballots were ferried overnight to the Counting Hall in Naxxar, where counting begins on Sunday morning.

item.title: Israel captures southern Lebanon fortress amid expanding operations
item.description: The Israeli army said on Sunday that it had captured the strategically located Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon and was expanding its ground operations against the Hezbollah militia in the area. A photo taken by a dpa reporter showed an Israeli flag being hoisted at the stronghold built by Crusaders in the 12th century.

item.title: Israel captures southern Lebanon fortress amid expanding operations
item.description: The Israeli army said on Sunday that it had captured the strategically located Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon and was expanding its ground operations against the Hezbollah militia in the area. A photo taken by a dpa reporter showed an Israeli flag being hoisted at the stronghold built by Crusaders in the 12th century.

item.title: Israel issues more displacement orders in Lebanon, seizes strategic castle
item.description: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz describes the capture of Beaufort Castle as a significant tactical victory.

item.title: Scotland's papers: SNP donors' 'court threat' and Sturgeon's 'England move'
item.description: A review of the front page stories from the daily newspapers in Scotland.

item.title: Israel plants flag on medieval castle, pushes Lebanon ground operation
item.description: Israel's flag flew over the medieval fortress on Beaufort in Lebanon on Friday as it warned Lebanese civilians to evacuate a large area of the south of the country ahead of stepped up ground operations. The push to Beaufort came as the Israeli military issued a sweeping evacuation order to areas south of the Zahrani River, north of the Litani and around 40 kilometres (25 miles) from the border, warning that it was targeting the Iran-backed Hezbollah armed group.

item.title: France detains hundreds during unrest after PSG Champions League win
item.description: Hundreds of people were detained across France following Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League victory over Arsenal, as celebrations in several cities turned into unrest. Speaking at a press conference on Sunday, French Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez said 416 people had been detained nationwide, including 283 in the Paris metropolitan area.

item.title: For Exposing China’s Repression, Women Are Targeted With Deepfake AI Porn
item.description: Many female activists who take on China are targeted by deepfake porn campaigns. Some are pushing back by publicizing the images.

item.title: Ukraine renews attacks on Russian oil facilities
item.description: Ukraine dealt another blow to Russia's oil industry overnight, launching drones at a storage depot by the southern city of Taganrog on the Sea of Azov and a refinery in Saratov on the Volga River, reports said on Sunday. Russian authorities partially confirmed the strikes, which were also reported by Ukrainian media.

item.title: Staff-Sergeant Michael Tyukin killed in combat in southern Lebanon
item.description: Four IDF soldiers were lightly wounded in the incident that killed Tyukin. Staff Sgt.

item.title: Missing Syrian chess champion’s children likely dead, authorities say
item.description: Syrian commission confirms deaths of Rania al-Abbasi’s six children, missing since 2013 under Bashar al-Assad’s rule.

item.title: The Mothers of May’s 20-year struggle for justice after Brazil police rampage
item.description: In 2006 police took revenge for deadly prison riots by killing more than 500 people in alleged shootouts that others call executions. A court is to rule on compensation for victims

item.title: Inside the schools preparing Ukrainian children for war
item.description: Valeriya was only 12 years old when the Russians invaded her homeland. Four and a half years on, she says she would never kill an animal, but would not hesitate to shoot a Russian soldier.

item.title: Israeli army captures strategic castle in Lebanon in deepest incursion into country in 26 years
item.description: Israeli troops have captured a strategic mountain topped with a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon in the deepest incursion of the country in more than a quarter-century, the military said Sunday. The capture of Beaufort castle, near the city of Nabatiyeh, came after days of airstrikes and intense fighting in nearby villages where Israeli troops fought Hezbollah members in the rugged area. Its capture marks a major development in the latest Israel-Hezbollah war, which began on March 2 when Hezbollah fired rockets into northern Israel two days after the U.S. and Israel attacked its main backer, Iran.

item.title: IDF pushes north past Litani River, holds Beaufort Ridge outpost for first time in 26 years
item.description: The operation began several days ago, and its goal is to eliminate the threat Hezbollah poses to the citizens of northern Israel. IDF ground troops have crossed the Litani River, entering and securing the Beaufort Ridge and Wadi al-Saluki areas of southern Lebanon, the military announced on Sunday morning.

item.title: Japan defence minister denies militarism, criticises China's 'huge arsenal'
item.description: Shinjiro Koizumi makes some of Tokyo's most pointed remarks yet in the dispute over Japan's military build-up.

item.title: Heritage plates aim to celebrate post box history
item.description: Humans sent by post, occupation stories and abrupt telegrams all feature in Guernsey's postal past.

item.title: Pilot taken to hospital after air show incident
item.description: Shuttleworth says the pilot was taken to hospital after giving "a thumbs up".

item.title: Japan defence chief takes swipe at China at security meet
item.description: Japan's defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing's criticism of Tokyo's increasingly muscular security stance.Japanese Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi hit back on Sunday, saying "nothing could be further from the truth".

item.title: Ancient shrine looks to future with upgrade plans
item.description: The charity that runs the site hopes to get the green light for improvements by early next year.

item.title: The Australian helping to return stolen English church artefacts
item.description: Items found for sale online had originally been stolen from a Hertfordshire and a Norfolk church.

item.title: How Nigeria’s ‘algorithmic apothecary’ fuels a surge in risky herbal cures
item.description: Unverified herbal remedies promoted on social media are driving rising health risks and delayed treatment in Nigeria.

item.title: Two decades later, impacts from Indonesia mud volcano linger
item.description: Harwati clasped her hands and offered a prayer on the edge of a sludge lake on the Indonesian island of Java, as dozen of residents gathered to mark two decades since mud volcano eruptions began displacing thousands.Harwati said the gathering, held on Friday to mark two decades since the disaster, was organised to remind the government that residents are still feeling the impact of the mudflow.

item.title: Iran war forces farmers to seek fertilizer alternatives from cow dung to compost
item.description: When Senegalese farmer Abou Sow first watched U.S. missiles strike Iran on social media, he had a sinking feeling it would soon affect agriculture in the West African nation. Since the war began on Feb. 28, fertilizer prices have risen by 40%. Eight years ago, he gave up chemical fertilizers for organic compost and other natural sources.

item.title: Deluded foreigners are blind to the reality of Cuba’s wretched regime
item.description: A former Cuban comandante, who had taken part in the Bay of Pigs on the Cuban side, once told me of his unease with his former communist beliefs. “If only the desire for human equality had been expressed as a spiritual quest – equality before God as it was in the Christian church – rather than as a political goal, a lot of conflict might have been averted.”

item.title: Japan defence chief takes swipe at China at security meet
item.description: Japan's defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing's criticism of Tokyo's increasingly muscular security stance.Japanese Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi hit back on Sunday, saying "nothing could be further from the truth".

item.title: Concerns mount that Belarus could be a launchpad for a new Russian offensive in Ukraine
item.description: Over four years ago, Belarus' authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko allowed longtime ally Russia to use his territory to invade Ukraine. Now officials in Kyiv are warning that Lukashenko could again allow his land to serve as a launchpad for more attacks by the Kremlin's forces. While Belarus has not contributed troops to battle, Lukashenko has backed President Vladimir Putin’s war effort by hosting Russia’s nuclear weapons and military infrastructure, as well as producing components for Moscow’s military industries.

item.title: Colombia's presidential election pits outgoing leader's ally against pro-Trump candidates
item.description: BOGOTÁ, Colombia (AP) — Colombians are casting ballots on Sunday in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential election, choosing between candidates with radically diverging visions for the future of peace in a country haunted by decades of armed conflict. The vote, seen as a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro’s policies, comes 10 years after Colombia signed an historic peace pact with guerrillas of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia, or FARC. Criminal groups have increasingly launched drone strikes, armed attacks have plagued the race and last June, 39-year-old politician and presidential hopeful Miguel Uribe Turbay was fatally shot at a political rally.

item.title: Hundreds march in Peru against right-wing presidential candidate
item.description: Hundreds of people marched in the streets of Peruvian capital Lima on Saturday in protest against right-wing presidential candidate Keiko Fujimori, eight days before a runoff vote in the South American country.Under the banner of "Keiko Out, Fujimori Never Again," the peaceful demonstrators held signs with slogans against the candidate and her late father, former Peruvian president Alberto Fujimori, an AFP journalist observed.

item.title: Mining turns India's heat-shield hills to dust
item.description: Dizzyingly deep pits from large-scale mining scar India's ancient Aravalli mountains, threatening the future of a forested buffer that New Delhi relies on for protection from furnace-hot desert winds."If we don't protect the Aravalli, the northern Gangetic plains -- which is a food basket for the rest of the country -- would become a desert," he said.

item.title: Colombian holds presidential election marred by violence
item.description: Colombians are voting for a new president on Sunday after an election campaign marred by tension and violence. Some 41 million people are eligible to vote for a successor to left-wing President Gustavo Petro, who is constitutionally barred from running for a second term.

item.title: Soaring prices during the Iran war jeopardize travel to tourism-dependent countries in Asia
item.description: With summer around the corner, soaring prices and other complications from the war with Iran are straining the tourism-dependent economies of countries in Southeast Asia, including Thailand and Vietnam. The region's peak tourist summer season is at risk as elevated jet fuel costs coupled with ceasefire uncertainties prompt flight cancellations and higher ticket prices. Tourism in Asia has yet to fully recover from the COVID-19 pandemic.

item.title: Zelensky expecting ‘big attacks’ from Russia soon
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is bracing for a major attack from Russia that he said he expects to occur within the next 48 hours, citing intelligence shared by the U.S. and European allies. “Today at night, or tomorrow at night, we will have, we think that we will have big attacks from Russian side using…

item.title: 'Totally hooked': Hong Kong targets claw machine addiction
item.description: Claw machine lover Neiki Lee carefully lowers the metal jaws of a crane with a joystick into a pool of prizes, only to have the small toy slip from its clutches again and again."For a HK$70 toy, you might eventually spend 700, or even 1,700, and still not be able to grab it," Lee told AFP. "I really want to give it up.

item.title: Kanye West concert cancelled over ‘public order and safety issues’
item.description: A Kanye West concert in Italy has been cancelled as a result of “public order and safety issues”.

item.title: UN adds Israeli and Russian forces to blacklist for sexual violence during conflict
item.description: The United Nations has added Israeli and Russian security forces for the first time to its annual blacklist documenting sexual violence during conflict, including allegations of rape against detained Palestinians. The report released Friday by the U.N. lists 77 government and non-government parties in countries “credibly suspected” of “patterns of rape or other forms of…

item.title: War or peace? Colombians choose destiny in high-stakes vote
item.description: Colombians vote Sunday in a presidential election that will determine the conflict-ridden nation's response to spiraling violence, either staying left and opting for dialogue or tacking right towards all-out war.- Right-wing rivals - Dialogue with guerrillas, however, is not to the taste of Cepeda's right-wing rivals, who are betting on security fears to shunt the left out of office.

item.title: Home Front Command issues new guidelines for the North, canceling school in Lebanon border towns
item.description: The new guidelines limit gatherings in communities along the Lebanese border to no more than 50 people outdoors and 200 indoors, and beaches are closed to the public. Home Front Command updated its security guidelines for Israel's northern communities on Saturday night, in light of the increasing tensions between Hezbollah and Israel.

item.title: How stamps and postcards helped India count its people
item.description: India's post office turned stamps, postcards and letters into tools for counting a nation after independence in 1947.

item.title: UK Foreign Secretary to visit China and India amid global crises
item.description: UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper will travel to China and India this week as the United Kingdom seeks to strengthen relations with both countries. Cooper will meet Chinese Vice President Han Zheng and Foreign Minister Wang Yi in Beijing on Tuesday, before visiting the southern city of Shenzhen, a major technology hub near Hong Kong.

item.title: How Putin became master of the image
item.description: From enigmatic KGB agent to wartime ruler, this is how Putin has repeatedly reinvented his image, and himself.

item.title: Actor Ian McKellen joins London march against LGBTQ+ laws abroad
item.description: British actor Ian McKellen launched a march against the criminalization of LGBTQ+ people in Commonwealth countries, branding it an "appalling situation. " The actor and activist joined protesters in central London to highlight laws in 29 Commonwealth countries where same-sex relationships remain illegal.

item.title: Nearly three years into war, Hezbollah keeps northern Israel under fire
item.description: Israeli cities remain under rocket fire in northern Israel after 965 days of conflict between Israel and Hezbollah. Israeli communities came under numerous attacks across northern Israel on May 30.

item.title: Man charged with murder of woman in County Galway
item.description: The victim, who was in her 30s, sustained slash and stab wounds in an attack earlier this week.

item.title: Arrests and clashes as tensions erupt in Paris on PSG victory night
item.description: Police on Saturday detained over 200 people in Paris after violent clashes erupted when thousands poured onto the streets during Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League final victory.According to the French interior ministry, 326 people were detained nationwide, including 235 who were apprehended in Paris.

item.title: Caribbean Food Security, One Year After The Collapse Of USAID
item.description: The abrupt collapse of USAID one year ago impacted food security, agriculture, and climate resilience across the Caribbean.

item.title: Thousands rally in western Germany against gas-fired power stations
item.description: Thousands of climate activists gathered in Hamm, in western Germany, to protest against gas-fired power plants on Saturday, in a largely peaceful gathering, police said. The rally, held under the banner, "Future instead of gas – defend the energy transition," opposed government plans to build new gas-fired power stations and called for an expansion of renewable energy.

item.title: Three dead, one rescued after fall on Alaska’s Mount McKinley
item.description: Three climbers from a Latvian mountaineering expedition died after falling near Denali Pass on Alaska’s Mount McKinley, North America’s highest peak.

item.title: 'It's like a decaying body': Australian farmers battle mouse plague
item.description: Farmers report thousands of mice per hectare destroying crops and invading homes.

item.title: Former premier of Turks and Caicos Islands is sentenced to 4 years in landmark corruption case
item.description: A court in the Turks and Caicos Islands has sentenced former Premier Michael Misick to just over four years in prison after he was convicted in a landmark corruption case that had dragged on for years. Misick was arrested in December 2012 in Brazil, after his application for political asylum was rejected there. Former Cabinet minister McAllister Hanchell and attorney Thomas Misick — Michael Misick's brother — were also sentenced in the same case on Friday.

item.title: Are Iranian-backed militias in Iraq really ready to hand over weapons?
item.description: A Kataib Hezbollah spokesperson said he “praised what he described as a decision by ‘[armed] brothers not involved in the Islamic Resistance [of Iraq]’ to end their armed activities. " Pressure is growing on the Iranian-backed militias in Iraq to hand over weapons to state authorities, or at least pretend to put their guns under state “control.

item.description: It's a major reminder that clean energy can strengthen security as well as cut pollution.

item.description: To sail through the strait without being detected by Iran, many ships are turning off their Automatic Identification Systems.

item.description: The US military said on Saturday it fired on a vessel in the Gulf of Oman that was violating the US naval blockade by approaching an Iranian port. The Gambia-flagged ship failed to comply with orders despite numerous warnings, the CENTCOM regional command said in a post on X, of the incident that occurred on Friday.

Hezbollah fired dozens of missiles and drones at the North over the weekend, with the IDF saying it was planning to intensify its campaign against the terrorist organization in Lebanon.

item.title: Zelensky renews warning of large-scale Russian attacks on Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky reiterated his warning about a new large-scale wave of Russian airstrikes on Saturday. “The intelligence report regarding a possible large-scale attack remains valid,” the president said in a video message a day after he initially sounded the alarm.

item.description: The garden housing the original Bramley apple tree has been sold, leaving campaigners aiming to turn it into a heritage site “gobsmacked”.

item.description: The mural was put up on a property in Tulleevin Walk on Saturday afternoon.

About 100 IDF veterans who were seriously injured in the Swords of Iron war are in the process of being admitted to Tel Aviv University without taking the psychometric exam, Walla reported on Wednesday.

item.description: Ukraine appears to be gaining momentum on the battlefield in its grinding fight with Russia, regaining territory for the first time in years as it outflanks Moscow’s forces through its domination of drone warfare. item.description: With support from the Magen 48 organization, Gaza border Kibbutz Nir Oz is training a new, young security team to protect their community. The paths here are lined with flowers and small bushes and even trees that provide a bit of shade.

item.description: Médicins Sans Frontières says that never before has an Ebola outbreak recorded so many cases so soon after its declaration.

item.description: The United States Navy’s USS Nimitz will arrive in Jamaica on Monday, the final overseas port call during the warship’s goodwill tour.

item.description: WHO’s Tedros calls for a community-led fight as a rare Ebola strain spreads rapidly through conflict-hit eastern DRC.

item.description: After 10 days trapped deep in a narrow cave, four Laotian men were able to walk out of the mine Saturday where rescuers were working to save them.

item.description: A Hezbollah missile hit the city center of Kiryat Shmona overnight, causing heavy damage to businesses; Iron Dome intercepted all other projectiles. The city center of Kiryat Shmona took a direct hit from a Hezbollah missile overnight, following a series of missile launches from Lebanon targeting Israel's northern communities, the IDF announced early Saturday morning.

item.description: Whitehall officials fear that Ireland has become a “back door” to Britain for Russian spies because of its generous visa programme, an Irish politician has said.

item.description: MATREI AM BRENNER, Austria, May 30 (Reuters) - Thousands of local residents shut down Austria's Brenner motorway on Saturday, a vital north-south corridor through the Alps between Germany and Italy,

item.description: There are currently 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths in the DRC.

item.title: Senior Sri Lankan monk suspended over child sex abuse allegation
item.description: Pallegama Hemarathana is accused of abusing an 11-year-old girl in a Buddhist temple in 2022.

item.title: Israel set to expand operations in southern Lebanon as clashes mount
item.description: Israel said on Saturday it is preparing for more intense rocket fire by the Lebanese Hezbollah militia, as the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) expand their operation in southern Lebanon. Earlier, the IDF ordered the evacuation of residents from 10 villages in Lebanon after renewed exchanges of fire with Iranian-backed Hezbollah.

item.title: 3 climbers killed in fall at Denali National Park
item.description: Three Latvian climbers died after a fall on Mount McKinley in Alaska. A fourth climber was rescued and hospitalized.

item.title: New Syrian chemical weapons find could help prosecute perpetrators of war crimes, experts say
item.description: Syrian Authorities have detained 18 individuals suspected of involvement in operating and managing the Assad regime’s chemical weapons program. The recent discovery of Assad-era chemical weapons munitions and materials in Syria has implications beyond the identification and destruction of hazardous stockpiles.

item.title: They were trained for a high-stakes underwater rescue. Instead, these villagers walked out of a cave to freedom
item.description: After a long and complex operation inside a flooded cave in a remote area of central Laos, on Saturday, the men at the center of the mission did something few expected: they walked out.

item.title: Israeli strikes reportedly pound near Crusader-built castle in Lebanon
item.description: Israel’s air force and artillery struck areas close to a strategic mountain housing a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon Saturday as fighting raged in villages close to the southern city of Nabatieh. Israel’s military issued evacuation warnings for more than a dozen villages in southern Lebanon, a day after Lebanese and Israeli military officials held their first direct talks in decades at the Pentagon. The situation in southern Lebanon was discussed during a meeting Saturday between Lebanon’s president and prime minister who said in a statement later that they will intensify their contacts to make Israel stop demolition and bulldozing of homes and historical sites as well as its evacuation warnings.

item.title: Cambridge students complain yellow exam papers hurt their eyes
item.description: Cambridge students have complained they are being examined in “crazy” conditions, citing yellow test papers and the danger of paper cuts.

item.title: Guyanese soldier wounded in border gunfight with armed men in Venezuela
item.description: A Guyanese soldier was wounded following a gunfight with armed men in Venezuela that occurred along their shared border, authorities said. The Guyana Defense Force said in a statement that a patrol vessel on the Cuyuni River came under fire late Friday. It is the latest of several bloody clashes in recent years as tensions remain high over Venezuela’s claim to two-thirds of Guyana’s territory.

item.title: Truck carrying Afghan returnees from Pakistan flips on highway, killing 18
item.description: At least 10 children among the dead as a packed truck carrying families returning from Pakistan flips on major highway.

item.title: Tens of thousands march in support of Turkey's deposed opposition leader
item.description: Ozgur Ozel was removed from his post at the head of the Republican People’s Party, or CHP, by court order on May 21. Many people consider the ruling to be a politically motivated bid to neutralize the opposition. Crowds earlier gathered in Guven Park in the heart of the Turkish capital to hear Ozel deliver a speech condemning his removal. “They are attempting to replace the CHP’s elected chairman and appoint a trustee,” Ozel told supporters.

item.title: Israeli soldiers reach Nabatieh, one of southern Lebanon’s biggest cities
item.description: Israel’s military has advanced beyond Lebanon’s Litani River for the first time since 2006.

item.description: The Himalayan roads put drivers to the ultimate test, given their narrow, winding sections and low oxygen levels caused by high altitude, making incidents a frequent occurrence. The footage of an incident involving a truck rolling backward and falling off…

item.title: French children found by side of road in Portugal return home
item.description: The two children from eastern France who were abandoned in Portugal have returned to their home country and been handed over to relatives there. The Colmar public prosecutor's office said on Saturday that the handover took place "under conditions that ensure their safety," French media reported.

item.title: Royal Caribbean drops mega water park plan after Mexico rejects permits over reef threat
item.description: The decision is a victory for residents and environmental advocates.

item.title: Influential French philosopher Edgar Morin dies at 104
item.description: Edgar Morin, one of the most influential French intellectuals of the modern era, has died in Paris at the age of 104. The philosopher and sociologist died on Friday, his family told French media.

item.description: "The trust is broken."

item.title: I’ve been shaken to the core by this window into the Oct 7 massacre
item.description: The terrorists laughed when they dragged Avi Dadon from the hole where he was hiding in the early hours of Oct 7 2023. Moments later, they forced his hands behind his back and stabbed him repeatedly in the chest. May Hayat bore witness to his murder. She had huddled in that pit near the Nova music festival with him, the strangers holding hands and praying as Hamas terrorists searched the area around them, closing in on their hiding place.

item.description: A Laos rescue organization said​ that the water level inside the cave had receded enough for the four miners to leave with divers.

item.description: Russian sources say Ukraine is now mining roads in the land corridor to Crimea with drones.

item.title: Thousands attend Austrian protest against congestion on key Alps route
item.description: Several thousand people gathered at the Brenner Pass on Saturday to protest growing road traffic along one of Europe’s busiest transport corridors linking northern and southern Europe. Demonstrators in the Austrian town of Matrei am Brenner carried signs reading “Enough is enough” and “Peace in the valley,” calling for measures to reduce the impact of heavy transit traffic on communities in the Alpine region.

item.title: Russia, Ukraine report casualties after fresh overnight attacks
item.description: Russia and Ukraine have traded another night of attacks, with both sides reporting casualties and damage on Saturday. The regional crisis management team in the Russian border region of Belgorod said three men were killed in Oktyabrsky, just a few kilometres from the border with Ukraine, during two attacks.

item.title: India says signed BrahMos missile deal with Vietnam
item.description: NEW DELHI, May 30 (Reuters) - India has signed a deal with Vietnam under which it will supply BrahMos missiles which it has jointly developed with Russia, and is in "final stages" for a similar deal

item.title: Teens arrested over 'racist attack' on man, 72
item.description: A 72-year-old man was hurt in the assault in Willenhall Memorial Park on Sunday, police say.

item.title: Ukraine keeps up assault on Russian oil infrastructure as Kyiv braces for more strikes
item.description: Ukrainian drone strikes caused fires at more Russian oil facilities overnight into Saturday, local Russian officials said, in what appeared to be the latest attack on Moscow’s vital oil industry. Authorities in Russia’s Rostov region said falling drone debris sparked a fire that damaged an oil depot and tanker in the port of Taganrog, while officials in the neighboring Krasnodar region reported a fire breaking out at an oil depot in Armavir for the same reason.

item.title: Israel launches more south Lebanon strikes after warnings
item.description: Israel launched strikes across south Lebanon Saturday after ordering evacuations from more than a dozen locations a day after its premier said Israeli forces had pushed even deeper into Lebanese territory.Lebanon's army said a "targeted" Israeli strike wounded two soldiers in the south, just a day after military delegations from both countries held landmark security talks in Washington.

item.title: 5 miners rescued from Laos cave, 2 still missing
item.description: Rescuers are still looking for two other missing villagers.

item.title: Munich Airport briefly halts flights after suspected drone sighting
item.description: Air traffic at Munich Airport was suspended for about an hour on Saturday morning following a suspected drone sighting. Take-offs and landings were halted shortly after 9 am (0700 GMT), but the alert was lifted at around 10:05 am, a spokesman for the German airport said.

item.title: WHO chief visits epicentre of Ebola outbreak in DR Congo
item.description: UN health chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus landed on Saturday in the eastern Democratic Republic of Congo province worst-hit by a severe Ebola outbreak, an AFP journalist saw. The World Health Organization's director general told reporters in Bunia, capital of Ituri province, that the international community was helping the DRC government cope with the outbreak, but "at the same time community ownership is important".

item.title: Egypt warns Israel that dangerous Gaza escalations threaten ceasefire
item.description: Egypt races to salvage Gaza ceasefire as Israeli attacks and displacement threats push deal to the brink of collapse.

item.title: Fire disrupts Brenner rail route amid closure of key Alpine road
item.description: A fire on a railway line north of the Italian city of Verona severely disrupted train services on the key Brenner route on Saturday, the local rail company said, compounding transport problems caused by the closure of the nearby Brenner Pass road crossing. Rail services between Peri and Dolcè on Lake Garda are affected, the state-owned railway company Ferrovie dello Stato said.

item.title: China threatens retaliatory measures if EU expands trade barriers
item.description: China has warned the European Union against imposing further trade restrictions following an internal EU discussion on relations with Beijing, saying it would respond firmly to any new measures it considers discriminatory. The Chinese Ministry of Commerce issued a statement on Saturday that the EU should uphold free trade and fair competition and reject protectionism and unilateral actions.

item.description: BANGKOK, May 30 (Reuters) - Rescuers pulled four people from a flooded cave in Laos on Saturday, Thai volunteer rescuers said, hours after another man was brought out late on Friday.
